name: Filet Mignon Sauce Madere
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle the meat on both sides with salt and pepper.
2. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over high heat until very hot.
3. When the oil is hot, add the shallots and cook for about 5 minutes, or longer if desired, turning occasionally to avoid burning.
4. Turn the meat and continue cooking over high heat for another 5 minutes, or longer if you prefer medium or well-done meat. Remove the meat to a warm platter and keep it warm. Pour off the fat from the skillet.
5. Add the wine and cook for about 30 seconds over high heat.
6. Add the beef broth and bring to a full rolling boil, stirring to dissolve any brown particles that cling to the bottom and sides of the skillet. Season with salt and pepper.
7. Reduce the sauce by half.
8. Meanwhile, blend 3 tbsp butter and flour in a small mixing bowl.
9. Stir this mixture into the simmering sauce over low heat for about 1 minute.
10. Swirl in the remaining tablespoon of butter.
11. Strain the sauce over the meat and dot the center of each filet with a dab of parsley. Serve immediately.
